cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for reporting/export and scope block
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row applies (FR-27, FR-28, BR-18, BR-19, AC-15, AC-16, AC-17).
Role-matrix fixture provides Lecturer assigned to section A, DepartmentAdmin scoped to faculty X, AcademicAdmin with institution scope, and Student account.
Attendance records exist in assigned section A, faculty Y outside department scope, and across institution.
steps: As Lecturer, call GET /v1/reports/attendance with termId and default pagination.
As DepartmentAdmin, call GET /v1/reports/attendance with faculty-scoped filters.
As AcademicAdmin, call GET /v1/reports/attendance with institution-wide term filter.
As Student, call GET /v1/reports/attendance with institution-wide filters.
Inspect each response for scope compliance and denial behavior on unauthorized actors.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row: Lecturer report rows limited to assigned sections; DepartmentAdmin limited to assigned faculty; AcademicAdmin within institution grant; Student receives denial without restricted data disclosure (BR-18, BR-19).

cases.title: M07 report query applies multi-filter predicates within real DB transaction
preconditions: Integration database seeded with attendance_records, class_sessions, enrollments, courses, and lecturer assignments.
M07 Reporting module uses real repository and DB transaction boundaries.
Attendance rows span multiple terms, sections, courses, lecturers, and statuses within actor scope.
steps: Invoke M07 getAttendanceReport use-case for Lecturer actor with filters { termId, classSectionId, status: Absent, from, to }.
Assert repository query applies allow-listed filter predicates before row materialization.
Verify returned row set contains only rows matching all filters and lecturer assigned classSectionId set.
Confirm zero rows from unassigned sections appear in committed query result.
cases.expected: Module integration returns scoped report rows matching term, section, status, and date-range filters; mandatory scope filters enforced at M07 boundary with no cross-section leakage (BR-18, VR-RP-02).

cases.title: M07 report repository enforces allow-listed sortBy and typed filter validation
preconditions: M07 report query service configured with server allow-list for sortBy fields (date, status, classSectionId).
Integration harness can invoke repository layer directly with filter and sort parameters.
steps: Invoke report query with sortBy=date and sortOrder=desc against seeded attendance data.
Verify SQL or repository ordering matches descending date sequence within scope.
Invoke report query with an unsupported sortBy value and observe validation rejection at module boundary.
Invoke report query with malformed filter type (non-UUID classSectionId) and observe InvalidFilter.
cases.expected: Allow-listed sort fields produce deterministic ordering; unsupported sortBy and malformed filter types are rejected at module boundary with InvalidFilter per VR-RP-01 before unrestricted query execution.

cases.title: HTTP contract for GET /v1/reports/attendance returns 200 paginated envelope
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated with valid Bearer token and assigned to a section with attendance data.
Active termId and classSectionId within lecturer scope are known.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&classSectionId={classSectionId}&page=1&pageSize=25 with Lecturer Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and standard { data, meta, error } response envelope.
Assert meta.pagination contains page, pageSize, totalItems, and totalPages per API §2.5.
Inspect data rows for student identifier, session reference, attendance status, and classSectionId fields.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 200 with standard envelope; meta.pagination reflects scoped totalItems; data array contains attendance report rows within lecturer assignment; error is null.

cases.title: HTTP contract for Lecturer POST /v1/auth/login then GET /v1/me before report query
preconditions: Provisioned Lecturer account with institution-issued credentials exists.
No active bearer token is held by the test client.
Attendance report data exists for lecturer assigned sections.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with lecturer email and password.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.accessToken, data.expiresInSeconds, and data.roles containing Lecturer.
Send GET /v1/me with Authorization Bearer accessToken from login response.
Assert HTTP 200 with lecturer identity and section scope claims required for report authorization.
Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 using the accessToken.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken and Lecturer role; GET /v1/me returns HTTP 200 with actor identity and scope claims; subsequent GET /v1/reports/attendance returns HTTP 200 with scoped rows only.

cases.title: HTTP contract for live roster payload aligns with scoped report rows
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Closed class session with mixed attendance outcomes exists in lecturer assigned section.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and roster envelope with data.classSessionId, data.counts, and data.rows[].
Assert each roster row includes studentCode, attendanceStatus, checkInMethod, and checkInAt per API §4.5.
Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId={sectionId}&from={sessionDate}&to={sessionDate} and compare report rows to roster entries for the same session.
cases.expected: Roster response is HTTP 200 with documented payload shape; report query for the same session returns matching student identifier, status, method, and timestamp values without additional cross-scope rows.

cases.title: HTTP contract for enrollment import establishes reportable rows with row-level rejection
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated with enrollment import permission.
Class section SE101-01 exists with lecturer assignment and closed sessions.
Import payload contains valid student rows and at least one invalid row (duplicate or malformed identifier).
steps: Send POST /v1/enrollments/import for SE101-01 with mixed valid and invalid rows.
Assert HTTP 200 or 207 with per-row acceptance and rejection details in response envelope.
Verify rejected rows are not enrolled and valid rows are persisted.
As assigned Lecturer, call GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId=SE101-01 and confirm only successfully enrolled students appear.
cases.expected: Enrollment import returns row-level rejection assertions for invalid rows; report query lists attendance rows only for successfully enrolled students in SE101-01 with no phantom entries from rejected import rows.

cases.title: Primary WF-07 workflow: lecturer filters and views paginated attendance report
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to section SE101-01 with closed sessions containing Present, Late, and Absent outcomes.
Another section SE101-02 in the same term has attendance data outside lecturer assignment.
Active term with known termId exists.
steps: Authenticate as Lecturer assigned to SE101-01.
Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&classSectionId=SE101-01&sortBy=date&sortOrder=desc&page=1&pageSize=25.
Apply status filter Absent on page 1 and verify row contents.
Navigate to page 2 if totalPages > 1 and verify pagination continuity.
cases.expected: End-to-end WF-07 report happy path succeeds: paginated in-app report returns only SE101-01 rows matching term, section, and status filters with correct sort order; no SE101-02 data appears (testing-plan E2E-08 report branch).

cases.title: Alternate path: DepartmentAdmin report filtered by course and lecturer within faculty scope
preconditions: DepartmentAdmin is authenticated and scoped to faculty X.
Faculty X contains multiple courses and lecturers with attendance data; faculty Y has separate data.
courseId and lecturerUserId values within faculty X are known.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&courseId={courseId}&lecturerUserId={lecturerId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as DepartmentAdmin.
Verify all returned rows belong to faculty X and match course and lecturer filters.
Call GET /v1/reports/attendance with classSectionId from faculty Y.
Inspect denial or empty scoped response for out-of-faculty filter.
cases.expected: DepartmentAdmin report succeeds with course and lecturer filters within assigned faculty; out-of-faculty classSectionId filter is denied or returns zero rows without faculty Y data leakage (BR-18 DepartmentAdmin branch).

cases.title: Report pagination invariants hold at module boundary across page boundaries
preconditions: Integration database seeded with more than 50 attendance report rows within a single lecturer scope.
M07 report query supports page and pageSize parameters with default pageSize 25.
steps: Query page 1 with pageSize=25 and record totalItems, totalPages, and row identifiers.
Query page 2 with same filters and pageSize=25.
Query last page using totalPages from first response.
Verify no duplicate row identifiers across pages and union of all pages equals totalItems within scope.
cases.expected: Pagination metadata is consistent: page >= 1, pageSize <= 100, totalPages = ceil(totalItems / pageSize); pages are non-overlapping and collectively cover all scoped rows; totalItems reflects scope-limited count only.

cases.title: E2E pagination metadata never exposes cross-scope totals
preconditions: Lecturer assigned to section A only; section B has substantially more attendance rows.
Both sections share the same term.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as Lecturer.
Record meta.pagination.totalItems and inspect data row classSectionId values.
Compare totalItems to known section A row count; verify it does not include section B volume.
cases.expected: meta.pagination.totalItems reflects only section A scoped count; no cross-scope row counts or section B identifiers appear in pagination metadata or data rows (API §2.5, BR-19).

cases.title: Student denied institution-wide attendance report
preconditions: Student user is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Institution-wide attendance report data exists across multiple sections.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as Student.
Validate HTTP status code and error envelope.
Inspect data and meta.pagination for any restricted attendance rows, student identifiers, or cross-scope totals.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 403 with error.code Forbidden or OutOfScope; data is null; meta.pagination is absent or contains no institution-wide totals; no partial attendance rows from other students are disclosed (BR-19, AC-16).

cases.title: Lecturer denied report query for unassigned classSectionId
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned only to section A.
Section B has attendance data and is not in lecturer assignment set.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId={sectionBId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as lecturer.
Inspect HTTP status, error.code, and response body for restricted data leakage.
Verify response contains no section B student identifiers or attendance statuses.
cases.expected: Request returns HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden; response contains no section B student identifiers, attendance statuses, or scoped pagination totals (PRM-10, BR-19).

cases.title: Unknown report filter parameter rejected with InvalidFilter error
preconditions: Lecturer or AcademicAdmin is authenticated with valid report permission.
VR-RP-01 requires allow-listed and typed query filters.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&unknownFilter=foo&page=1&pageSize=25.
Validate HTTP status and error envelope.
Confirm no report rows are returned in data array.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 400 or 422 with error.code InvalidFilter; error details identify the unsupported filter key; data is null with no partial report rows.

cases.title: Report pageSize above maximum 100 rejected
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated with institution report access.
Pagination constraint: 1 <= pageSize <= 100 per validation rules §4.1.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=101.
Validate HTTP status and error envelope.
Retry with page=0 and observe rejection.
cases.expected: pageSize=101 returns HTTP 400 with InvalidPayload or validation error; page=0 is rejected; no report data returned for invalid pagination parameters.

cases.title: Out-of-scope classSectionId filter rejected before report query executes
preconditions: DepartmentAdmin scoped to faculty X is authenticated.
Attendance data exists in faculty Y section outside department scope.
steps: Submit GET /v1/reports/attendance with classSectionId targeting faculty Y.
Parse 403 response and any error.details field.
Verify no attendance rows from faculty Y appear in data or pagination metadata.
cases.expected: Scope validation short-circuits with HTTP 403 OutOfScope before unrestricted query; error.details contain no faculty Y attendance samples, student identifiers, or row counts (VR-RP-02, BR-19).

cases.title: Lecturer browser journey filters, sorts, searches, and paginates PG-13 attendance report
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
PG-13 /reports/attendance is reachable with TableToolbar and DataTable components.
Attendance data exists in assigned and unassigned sections for the active term.
steps: Navigate to /reports/attendance and confirm term and section filter options list only assigned sections.
Apply status filter Absent and date range from/to; verify table updates.
Enter student name or code in search field and confirm filtered results.
Click date column header to toggle sortOrder; navigate to page 2 via pagination controls.
Verify URL query params sync with toolbar state (termId, classSectionId, status, from, to, sortBy, sortOrder, page, pageSize, search).
cases.expected: PG-13 listing implements search, filter, sort, and server pagination per §0 matrix; toolbar state syncs with URL; displayed rows remain within lecturer assigned scope with AttendanceStatusCell badges; no unassigned section data appears.

cases.title: Browser journey shows empty, no-results, and access-denied states on report listing
preconditions: Lecturer and Student test accounts available in browser harness.
Lecturer has assigned section with attendance data; Student lacks institution report permission.
steps: As Lecturer, apply filters that match zero rows and observe no-results state with clear-filters affordance.
As Lecturer, select a section with no attendance records and observe empty state.
As Student, attempt direct navigation to /reports/attendance.
Observe permission empty or access-denied state without partial attendance table data.
cases.expected: Listing page states per NFR-UI-07: no-results shows localized message and clear-filters action; empty state shows setup guidance; Student sees access denial without restricted attendance rows or cross-scope counts (AC-UI-09, BR-19).
